name: has
description: The standalone lodash.has package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want lodash-style deep property existence checks without an npm dependency.
tagline: Check whether a deep path exists.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.has file in both raw and normalized forms. The value is to vendor a known path-existence contract instead of rewriting the check by hand.
nativeAlternative: Optional chaining can help with reads, but path existence with dynamic segments and lodash-style semantics is not always a one-line native replacement.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
